merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established with Prussia when John Quincy Adams was formally recognized as the accredited U.S. Minister to King Frederick William III during a private audience.

evidence.quote: During a private audience on December 5, however, the new king, Frederick William III, formally recognized Adams as the accredited U.S. Minister to his father.

merged_events.event_description: Legation elevated to Embassy when Theodore Runyon was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented credentials.

evidence.quote: Theodore Runyon, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1893-03-23. Credentials presented: 1893-06-04. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1893-10-26. Note: Commissioned to Germany.

merged_events.event_description: The United States severed diplomatic relations with Germany; Ambassador Gerard suspended official functions and left post.

evidence.quote: The United States severed diplomatic relations with Germany on 1917-02-03. Note: Commissioned to Germany. Gerard received official notice of the suspension of relations on Febraury 5, 1917, and he suspended his official functions. He left post on February 10, 1917.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations with Germany resumed; Ellis Loring Dresel presented credentials as Chargé d'Affaires, re-establishing the Embassy.

evidence.quote: Ellis Loring Dresel, Chargé d'Affaires. Appointed: 1921-11-18. Credentials presented: 1921-12-10. Left post on 1922-04-18. Note: Commissioned to Germany.

merged_events.event_description: Germany declared war on the United States, ending all diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: Leland Burnette Morris, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1940-10. Germany declared war on the United States on 1941-12-11.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. Embassy in Germany was reestablished on May 5, 1955, with Ambassador-designate Conant (previously serving as High Commissioner) in charge.

evidence.quote: The Embassy in Germany had been reestablished May 5, 1955, with Ambassador-designate Conant (who had been serving as High Commissioner) in charge except for a brief temporary absence pending confirmation of his nomination, commission, and presentation of his letter of credence.

matched.notes: Exact date and status match. Both record establishment of Legation on 1797-12-05 when John Quincy Adams was recognized as Minister to Prussia.
Exact date and status match. POCOM records promotion of Theodore Runyon to Ambassador on 1893-10-26, marking elevation to Embassy.
Exact date and status match. U.S. severed diplomatic relations with Germany on 1917-02-03.
Exact date and status match. Diplomatic relations resumed on 1921-12-10 when Dresel presented credentials as Chargé d'Affaires.
Exact date and status match. Germany declared war on the United States on 1941-12-11.

discrepancies.reasoning: Both refer to the reestablishment of the U.S. Embassy in Germany. The POCOM source explicitly states 'The Embassy in Germany had been reestablished May 5, 1955, with Ambassador-designate Conant (who had been serving as High Commissioner) in charge.' No RDCR event provides a competing institutional date for this transition. The CSV date of May 14 may reflect a different event such as the presentation of credentials or another administrative milestone, but the institutional reestablishment date per POCOM is May 5, 1955. Since RDCR does not provide a specific institutional date, POCOM's date should be preferred per source priority rules.
